citations.description: No autobump.yml or any reference to navapbc/homebrew-rebar automation found in any file accessible in this repository via search_files('autobump') and search_files('homebrew').

findings.criterion: Add a scheduled + workflow_dispatch self-bump workflow (autobump.yml) in the tap repo (navapbc/homebrew-rebar) that polls PyPI, updates Formula/rebar.rb url+sha256, and commits with GITHUB_TOKEN (no stored cross-repo token); idempotent no-op when current.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged (not [operator-attested]) so it must be verified in the codebase. The navapbc/homebrew-rebar tap repo is a different repository, outside the repo tree accessible by my tools. No evidence of autobump.yml or any related workflow file is findable within this repository. The completion comment cites commit 9d1e594 and run IDs, but without the operator-attested tag those attestations cannot substitute for codebase evidence. This criterion cannot be verified as met.
findings.remediation: Either tag this criterion as [operator-attested] and record a verifiable attestation (tap commit URL, workflow run URL, and observed outcome) as a ticket comment, or add any cross-repo evidence document within this repo. Currently the tap repo is inaccessible and no codebase evidence exists.

findings.criterion: Update docs/releasing.md step 3 to mark Homebrew automated (manual = fallback).
findings.detail: docs/releasing.md section '3. Update the Homebrew tap' (lines 310-326) still contains only the fully manual recipe (curl + edit + brew style + git commit/push). There is no mention of automation, 'scheduled self-bump', or 'manual = fallback'. The text is identical to the pre-ticket manual runbook.

findings.criterion: Release v0.9.1 — pyproject.toml + server.json bumped to 0.9.1; CHANGELOG [0.9.1] present.
findings.detail: pyproject.toml still reads version = "0.9.0" (line 10) and server.json reads "version": "0.9.0" (lines 5, 14). No [0.9.1] entry exists in CHANGELOG.md (latest is [0.9.0] at line 11). A search for '0.9.1' across the repository returns only an unrelated test file match. The version bump to 0.9.1 has not landed in this repo.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
